# Moroccan Chicken Tagine with Apricots
NOTE: This is an AI-generated test document, it's not real, and any resemblance to real people or places is coincidence.

Picked this up after a trip to Marrakech, and refined it over a few tries with input
from Chef Anong (of all people) who pointed out the spice ratios were off.

## Ingredients
- 4 chicken thighs and 4 drumsticks
- 2 tbsp olive oil
- 1 onion, sliced thin
- 3 cloves garlic, minced
- 1 tsp ground ginger
- 1 tsp ground cumin
- 1 tsp ground cinnamon
- 0.5 tsp turmeric
- 0.25 tsp saffron threads
- 1.5 cups chicken stock
- 1 cup dried apricots
- 0.5 cup green olives
- 0.25 cup toasted almonds
- Fresh cilantro, chopped
- Couscous, for serving

## Instructions
1. Season chicken with salt and pepper, brown in olive oil in a tagine or heavy pot.
   Remove and set aside.
2. Sauté onion and garlic in the same pot until soft.
3. Stir in ginger, cumin, cinnamon, turmeric, and saffron, toast 30 seconds.
4. Return chicken to the pot, add stock, bring to a simmer.
5. Cover and cook on low for 40 minutes.
6. Add apricots and olives, cook uncovered another 15 minutes until sauce thickens.
7. Scatter almonds and cilantro over the top, serve with couscous.

## Notes
Chef Anong suggested blooming the saffron in a splash of warm stock before adding it,
which noticeably deepens the color and flavor versus adding it dry.
